The target remains exactly the over-G isomorphism question stated in the prompt: whether there is a continuous ő¶:őďS‚ÜíőďH with ŌÄH‚ąėő¶=ŌÄS.

The frozen proof chain remainsH1 -Nielsen¬†equivalence+¬†residual¬†restricted-Lie¬†induction+¬†normalized¬†Koszul‚ÄďWitt¬†coefficient¬†induction+D.1‚ÄďD.4+¬†finite¬†filtered¬†Nielsen¬†compactness‚üĻőďS‚ČÖGőďH.The compactness descent is still the final global mechanism: finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle data give compatible finite over-G automorphisms; their inverse limit gives ő¶:F‚ÜíF with ő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH; the descended quotient map satisfies ŌÄH‚ąėő¶=ŌÄS.The measured-certificate coordinate also remains unchanged. History-recorded verifier claims are ledger evidence for the theorem state, but they are not themselves measured input for the strict Certificate D gate.

So the strict gate remains:

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. PASS would require genuine object-level measured D.1‚ÄďD.4 artifacts matching the frozen contract; FAIL would require genuine measured artifacts contradicting it; a ledger-derived expected-values file would be rejected as non-measured.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 103 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 102. The theorem coordinate remains őďS‚ČÖGőďH, via an over-G automorphism ő¶:F‚ÜíF satisfying ő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH. The measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.
